Traders' luck is judged from Suvarnapufpa (golden flower);
Brahmanas, from lotuses; royal priests, from lilies; the commander-
in-chief of the army, from the Saugandhika flower; and the increase
of gold, from the Arka plant.
One should infer the happiness of mankind from mangoes;
danger, from Bhallataka; health, from Pilu; famine from the growth
of Khadifa and Sami trees; and good rain, from the Arjuna trees.
5^ n^u
From the flowers of the Neem tree and Nngakesara, abundance
of food grains can be predicted; from the wond«apple, wind; from
the Jficula tree, danger of drought; and from the Kutaja tree, threat
of diseases.
nn»
